articles.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
articles.summary: Tech companies are rapidly expanding AI data centers as foundational infrastructure, but this growth is sparking global conflicts over energy consumption, power grids, utility costs, and environmental impact.

articles.title: PlayStation sees AI as a ‘powerful tool’ to help make games
articles.summary: Sony views AI as a powerful tool for PlayStation game development while maintaining that human vision, design, and emotional impact remain central to their games.

articles.title: Microsoft was worried OpenAI would run off to Amazon and ‘shit-talk’ Azure
articles.summary: Court documents reveal Microsoft executives feared OpenAI would leave for Amazon and criticize Azure. The concerns emerged during early partnership discussions between Microsoft CEO Nadella and OpenAI CEO Altman in 2017.

articles.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
articles.summary: Oracle laid off workers who attempted to negotiate better severance terms but were refused. Some employees were denied WARN Act protections due to remote worker classification.

articles.title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
articles.summary: Intel's stock surged 490% over the past year as Wall Street bets on its turnaround. However, this rally may be overestimating the company's actual recovery progress.

articles.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
articles.summary: Cloudflare laid off 1,100 employees due to AI efficiency gains, particularly in support roles, despite hitting record revenue. CEO Matthew Prince attributed the cuts to improved automation capabilities.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure competing with AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI-powered coding, but Goose offers free, open-source alternative with similar features that runs locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI-encoded tokens and coding challenges. The unconventional recruitment stunt helped attract top engineering talent in a competitive hiring market.

articles.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
articles.summary: A cyberattack disrupted Canvas learning platform during US finals, forcing schools offline. Instructure restored service Friday after identifying unauthorized network activity, with user data including names and emails compromised.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.summary: Mozilla's AI tool Mythos discovered 271 Firefox vulnerabilities with minimal false positives over two months. The breakthrough demonstrates practical AI-assisted vulnerability detection effectiveness, addressing skepticism about AI security claims.

articles.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
articles.summary: Terminal windows have become increasingly important despite predictions of graphical interfaces replacing command lines. The command line remains superior for many tasks compared to point-and-click interfaces.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
articles.summary: In week two of Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, the company fired back, claiming Musk pushed for a for-profit structure and is now suing due to losing control. Shivon Zilis testified that Musk attempted to recruit Sam Altman.

articles.title: Here’s what you need to know about the cruise ship hantavirus outbreak
articles.summary: Eight passengers on a Dutch cruise ship contracted hantavirus, a rare virus transmitted by rats, with three fatalities. The ship is docking in the Canary Islands to safely disembark remaining passengers and crew.

articles.title: The Download: AI malaise and babymaking tech
articles.summary: Society faces uncertainty about AI's widespread integration, questioning whether it will improve or harm lives, jobs, and the economy. There's growing discomfort as AI becomes unavoidable in everyday applications without clear understanding of its long-term impact.

articles.title: CyberSecQwen-4B: Why Defensive Cyber Needs Small, Specialized, Locally-Runnable Models
articles.summary: CyberSecQwen-4B is a specialized small AI model designed for cybersecurity defense that runs locally without requiring large computational resources, enabling faster threat detection and response.

articles.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
articles.summary: EMO introduces a pretraining approach for mixture of experts models that develops emergent modularity. This method enables more efficient specialization of expert networks during training.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM upgraded from V0 to V1, prioritizing correctness in reinforcement learning before applying corrections. The update ensures more reliable model outputs through improved validation methods.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development. The conversation explores upcoming trends and innovations shaping the next phase of artificial intelligence advancement.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing initiatives.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools available to developers and farmers for advancing sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
articles.summary: Google launches The Small Brief, partnering creative advertising legends with local businesses to create innovative AI-powered ads. The initiative aims to showcase how established industry icons can help small businesses grow through cutting-edge advertising.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google Search now offers gardening tips using AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features to help users grow thriving plants.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners to launch the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition, encouraging filmmakers to explore AI's potential impact on society.

articles.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
articles.summary: AlphaEvolve leverages Gemini-powered algorithms to create coding agents that scale impact across business, infrastructure, and scientific fields.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. This new model aims to enhance clinical care through AI collaboration with healthcare professionals.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using frontier AI models.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk's testimony dominated the first week of his lawsuit against Sam Altman, with disputes over OpenAI's charitable status and allegations of model misuse by xAI.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: Episode 243 discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with coding improvements and chain-of-thought monitoring, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #242 discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's improved ChatGPT image model with better text generation, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6 updates.

articles.title: RingCentral adds Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp to AI Receptionist
articles.summary: RingCentral expanded its AI Receptionist product with integrations for Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp, enabling it to handle orders, appointments, and messages. Over 11,800 businesses now use the AI tool for customer service and after-hours support.

articles.title: AI helping ease the UK’s NHS burden
articles.summary: AI-enabled virtual care is emerging as a solution to help the UK's NHS manage its 7.25 million patient waiting list by shifting care from hospitals to community settings, addressing capacity and staffing challenges.

articles.title: HP and the art of AI and data for the enterprise
articles.summary: HP discusses leveraging enterprise data for AI advantage, addressing challenges in data processing and the choice between cloud-hosted versus local compute solutions for business applications.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed automated failure attribution methods for LLM multi-agent systems to identify which agent causes task failures and when, improving collaboration in complex problem-solving.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to update their own weights through self-editing and reinforcement learning, advancing toward self-improving AI systems.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables LLMs to handle 100 million token context windows while maintaining accuracy, representing a major breakthrough in scaling language model memory capacity.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are inadvertently leaking sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, posing significant supply chain risks.

articles.title: OpenAI Adds Chrome Extension to Codex, Letting Its AI Agent Access LinkedIn, Salesforce, Gmail, and Internal Tools via Signed-In Sessions
articles.summary: OpenAI launched a Codex Chrome extension enabling its AI agent to access LinkedIn, Salesforce, Gmail, and other tools through signed-in browser sessions, filling gaps previous API and plugin approaches couldn't cover.

articles.title: How to Build a Single-Cell RNA-seq Analysis Pipeline with Scanpy for PBMC Clustering, Annotation, and Trajectory Discovery
articles.summary: Tutorial on single-cell RNA-seq analysis using Scanpy for PBMC clustering and annotation, covering quality control, normalization, dimensionality reduction, cell clustering, marker identification, and trajectory analysis.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Natural Language Autoencoders That Convert Claude’s Internal Activations Directly into Human-Readable Text Explanations
articles.summary: Anthropic introduced Natural Language Autoencoders (NLAs) that convert Claude's internal activations into human-readable text explanations. This advancement makes AI model reasoning more interpretable and accessible to non-researchers.

articles.title: Don't connect your smart plug to these 5 household devices - an expert warns
articles.summary: Smart plugs offer convenience but shouldn't be used with certain household devices. An expert warns against connecting five specific items to smart plugs for safety reasons.

articles.title: Worried about the nationwide Canvas data breach? Take these 6 steps now
articles.summary: A ransomware group claims to have stolen 275 million records from Canvas affecting students, teachers, and staff. The article provides six steps to protect yourself following the breach.

articles.title: Windows rivals to MacBook Neo are arriving - but can you handle their shortcomings?
articles.summary: Budget PCs are catching up to MacBook Neo but still have shortcomings. Chromebooks may be better positioned to compete in the affordable laptop market.

articles.title: Implementing Permission-Gated Tool Calling in Python Agents
articles.summary: AI agents now use permission-gated tool calling in Python, enabling secure execution of actions. This advancement moves beyond passive chatbots toward autonomous, controlled agents.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Tool Calling in AI Agents
articles.summary: The article provides a guide for mastering tool calling capabilities in AI agents, enabling them to effectively use external tools and APIs to accomplish complex tasks.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: Non-deterministic agents produce varying outputs for identical inputs. The article discusses implementing statistical guardrails to ensure reliable and consistent performance across multiple runs.

articles.title: From Data Scientist to AI Architect
articles.summary: The article discusses the shift from model-centric thinking to a broader AI architecture approach in data science, marking an evolution in how professionals approach AI development.

articles.title: The AI Agent Security Surface: What Gets Exposed When You Add Tools and Memory
articles.summary: AI agents with tools and memory expose new security vulnerabilities beyond standard prompt attacks. A framework is needed to identify and mitigate backend attack vectors in agentic workflows.

articles.title: When Customers Churn at Renewal: Was It the Price or the Project?
articles.summary: Guide to determining causal attribution when customers churn at renewal due to multiple factors like pricing or project issues.

articles.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
articles.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck argue that American AI leadership depends on energy leadership, with AI helping to power its own energy needs.

articles.title: Linked and Loaded: Gaijin Single Sign-On Now Available on GeForce NOW
articles.summary: GeForce NOW now supports Gaijin single sign-on, allowing faster logins for cloud gaming. Seven new games were added to the platform this week.

articles.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI, Now With MRC
articles.summary: NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet fabric is the leading AI networking technology for large-scale AI infrastructure, now deployed by OpenAI, Microsoft, and Oracle.

articles.title: Halliburton enhances seismic workflow creation with Amazon Bedrock and Generative AI
articles.summary: Halliburton partnered with AWS to develop an AI-powered assistant using Amazon Bedrock that simplifies seismic workflow creation, reducing manual configuration complexity for energy exploration data processing.

articles.title: Secure short-term GPU capacity for ML workloads with EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans
articles.summary: AWS introduces EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans to address GPU scarcity, enabling companies to secure reliable short-term GPU capacity for machine learning workloads amid high demand.

articles.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI
articles.summary: A new approach using verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning (RLVR) addresses reliability issues in LLM training by introducing verification and transparency into reward signals for tasks like math and code generation.

articles.title: Improving Bash Generation in Small Language Models with Grammar-Constrained Decoding
articles.summary: Grammar-constrained decoding improves Bash command generation in small language models, enabling AI agents to produce valid shell commands for file operations and system tasks.

articles.title: Streaming Tokens and Tools: Multi-Turn Agentic Harness Support in NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo introduces streaming tokens and tools support for multi-turn agentic interactions, enabling structured exchanges where assistant reasoning interleaves with tool calls and user responses.

articles.title: Achieving Peak System and Workload Efficiency on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 with Slurm Block Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 extends NVLink coherence across entire racks for exascale performance, requiring new scheduling approaches like Slurm block scheduling to optimize cluster efficiency and workload management.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study explores how different generations perceive social media ads across platforms, examining consumer attitudes toward advertising on social media.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these solutions openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology. Their unique perspective and diverse experience enabled breakthrough developments in XR.

articles.title: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning: The Next Paradigm in Efficient Inference Scaling
articles.summary: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning enables AI models to autonomously decide when to decompose tasks, spawn concurrent threads, and coordinate them based on problem requirements, advancing efficient inference scaling.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models that enables long-horizon planning through virtual state lifting, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping to improve optimization across high-dimensional visual spaces.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers propose methods to identify and understand interactions at scale in Large Language Models, improving interpretability by analyzing feature attribution and decision-making processes for safer AI systems.
